note: Scenario contract for decoupling the CI mobile-client build from the SERVER deploy manifest. SCN-098-A01 (non-release server-only manifest) + A02 (release still pins clients) + A03 (drift detector enforces the conditional contract) are unit-proven under SCOPE-01 by the lockstep drift-detector test internal/deploy/build_workflow_clients_contract_test.go (live-file + policy + adversarial sub-tests). SCN-098-A04 (deployment docs describe the server-only manifest + knb-gate expectation) is a docs-review scenario under SCOPE-02 evidenced in docs/Deployment.md. Every scenario is linked 1:1 to its owning scope and a report.md evidence anchor.

scenarios.title: Non-release push publishes a server-only manifest
scenarios.given: no ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 secret is configured and the workflow runs on a push to main (not a tag, no build_clients override)
scenarios.then: build-clients is skipped, publish-build-manifest still runs once the server-side jobs succeed, and a server-only build-manifest (core + ml + bundles + chrome-bridge) is published with the android platform NOT contracted

scenarios.title: Tagged release still builds and pins the clients
scenarios.given: the operator-private Android signing secrets are configured and the workflow runs on a tag push (refs/tags/v*) or build_clients=true dispatch
scenarios.then: build-clients builds + signs + pushes the AAB + APK by digest, publish-build-manifest pins them under clients.artifacts[], and a build-clients FAILURE on the release still blocks the manifest

scenarios.title: Drift detector enforces the new conditional contract
scenarios.given: the lockstep contract test parses the live build.yml
scenarios.when: it asserts the conditional decoupling contract
scenarios.then: the release-gate, skip-tolerance, and success-gate are accepted, while an adversarial mutation removing any of them is rejected, and a non-release manifest is accepted without android digests while a release manifest without android digests is rejected

scenarios.title: Deployment docs describe the server-only manifest contract
scenarios.when: they look up what CI publishes on a non-release push
scenarios.then: the docs state a server-only manifest (no android platform contracted) is published and promotable, state the knb-side expectation that a server-only manifest must not contract android so E025-CLIENT-MANIFEST-NO-DIGEST has nothing to fail-close on, and note the knb adapter is operator-private and out of repo scope
